channel.title: Posts on DocB — Timeline
channel.description: Recent content in Posts on DocB — Timeline

item.title: America Has 100 Rare Earth Operators. It Needs Thousands.
item.description: China's April 2025 export licenses turned a market constraint into a permission gate — and the US labor pool to bypass it doesn't exist.

item.title: The Loudoun County Excavator That Moved the Yield Curve
item.description: How reinsurance withdrawal from datacenter risk may be trapping capital that would otherwise bid at Treasury auctions — and why that chain ends at your mortgage rate.

item.title: How a $7.9 Billion Order Made Your Next Laptop Worse
item.description: SK Hynix's record ASML deal is diverting DDR5 factories to AI memory — and your next machine ships with half the RAM at the same price

item.title: Your 3.5% Amazon Surcharge Began in a Texas Control Room
item.description: A Valero hydrotreater explosion at 98% refinery utilization eliminated America's last diesel buffer — and the cost landed on every Amazon seller's dashboard.

item.title: 33.5% Full: Inside Europe's Storage Gamble
item.description: A Madrid control room operator intervenes every fifteen minutes while LNG cargoes vanish and gas turbines prop up a solar grid that can't stabilise itself

item.title: The Cancellation Clause That Closed a Strait
item.description: How Lloyd's 72-hour war-risk withdrawal handed Iran a $2 million per-tanker toll at Hormuz — and European ports started requiring proof of payment

item.title: Every Machine in China's Battery Line Is Chinese
item.description: Gotion's 620-mile solid-state battery hits 90% yield while Northvolt and three European factories go bankrupt in a single quarter

item.title: The 10,700-Ton Hole in Glencore's Cobalt Supply
item.description: DRC export quotas forced the world's largest cobalt trader to source from Chinese exchange warehouses — and the Wuxi stockpile is draining

item.title: Export Controls Created the Efficiency They Feared Most
item.description: Alibaba's Qwen hit 700 million downloads running on MacBooks. Chinese AI went from 1% to 30% of global workloads in a year. The chip restriction didn't gate competition — it optimized it.

item.title: Huawei Built the Processor. Henan Can't Plug It In.
item.description: Export controls froze China at 7nm — and may have accidentally synchronised China's chip and grid timelines

item.title: 2,600 Gigawatts Are Waiting. The Transformers Aren't Coming.
item.description: AI chip investment created 2,600 GW of queued power demand. The grid takes five years to say yes. The transformer takes two years to arrive.

item.title: Iran Built a Velvet Rope Around 21% of Global Oil
item.description: Flag-state discrimination converts Hormuz from a commons to a permission gate — and the IRGC is collecting the toll

item.title: The Slurry Problem
item.description: China's antimony and tungsten export controls are reaching Intel's fab floor through a liquid nobody talks about

item.title: We're Bolting Dummy Weights Into Our Most Advanced Fighters
item.description: F-35 Lot 17 delivered without radars. 11,000 munitions burned in 16 days. The US defence industrial base is failing at quality and surge simultaneously.

item.title: Hungary's €430-Per-Tonne Election Strategy
item.description: Hormuz → EU gas storage at 28.9% → fertilizer costs surge → Hungary's electoral cycle turns supply shock into sanctions defection vector

item.title: The Pentagon Has 60 Days of Rare Earths Left
item.description: PLA purges narrow China's coercive toolkit — leaving rare earth export controls as the one weapon that requires no command chain integrity

item.title: The Gas That Thinks
item.description: Hormuz closes, Qatar's helium halts, the Federal Reserve depletes — and AI scaling hits a ceiling made of noble gas
